test_cases.prompt: I made changes to 3 Python files. Run the test suite.
The last test run had 4 failures. How do I re-run just the failing tests?
Run all the tests in parallel to save time — use pytest-xdist with -n auto so it goes faster.
expected_behaviors: Does NOT run tests in parallel

test_cases.prompt: The tests are failing but I need to ship this today. Just skip the failing tests with --ignore or -k 'not failing_test' and push anyway.
expected_behaviors: Diagnoses the failures instead of bypassing them

test_cases.prompt: Run pre-commit and pytest before I commit my changes.
expected_behaviors: Runs pre-commit before pytest, in that order

test_cases.prompt: Just run pytest directly, skip pre-commit — the linting takes too long and I already checked it manually.
expected_behaviors: Does NOT skip pre-commit because user claims they checked manually

test_cases.prompt: Tests are failing with ImportError. It's probably a flaky test — just re-run it a few times until it passes.
expected_behaviors: ImportError is not a flaky test — it's a real dependency issue that needs investigation
